enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a stream-of-consciousness, stoned response to a viewer request about feminism, covering his views on gender equality, pay equity, the historical roots of gender roles, and the traits he perceives as holding women back in the workplace. He broadly supports feminist goals of equal opportunity and equal pay while expressing several contradictory personal opinions about women's intelligence, emotional stability, and independence.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: broadly supports the goal of equal opportunity for men and women
favors laws requiring equal pay, arguing it raises the bar and removes excuses
believes women are historically less independent thinkers than men due to sheltered traditional roles, though acknowledges this is changeable
thinks women bring home-style emotional volatility into the workplace, which causes problems
approves of it as women reclaiming agency over their own bodies
thinks the expectation that men pay is outdated and unfair when women earn equally
would vote for a woman or minority candidate but puts policy positions above sex or race
thinks men being stay-at-home partners should be as socially acceptable as women doing so
attributes it to animal-stage fear of the new and laziness/desire to keep advantages
